name: Tool-Making and Self-Evolving LLM Agents in Low-Latency Systems

four_principles_filter.去粗取精: 研讨厅按 intake_score≥0.6 + 子系统匹配入库
four_principles_filter.去伪存真: 代码级落地/规划态已在决策中标注
four_principles_filter.由表及里: 研讨厅驱动正式入库固化

digest_notes: Production LLM agents often waste latency and reliability by regenerating code for the same procedural steps. We replace this inference-time coding loop with an agentic tool-making pipeline that compiles repeated SOP steps into validated, versioned tools before deployment. The tool-maker grounds synthesis in the live environment as it collects execution traces. In production alarm-triage, tool calls reduce p50 latency by 42% and reduce end-to-end error rate by up to 53%.
